---
title: "R packages"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
execute:
  echo: false
  fig-width: 6
  fig-asp: 0.6
  out-width: "90%"
---



## Overview

In this, we will learn about writing R packages.

```{r, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(microbenchmark)
```

::: {.panel-tabset .panel-pills}

### Slide Deck: Introduction to R packages

[![](./slides/topic_R_packages/preview_package.png){width=70%}](./slides/topic_R_packages/s_packages.pdf)

**Intro R packages**
[Download slides (PDF)](./slides/topic_R_packages/s_packages.pdf)


***

### Slide Deck: Rcpp

[![](./slides/topic_R_packages/preview_rcpp.png){width=70%}](./slides/topic_R_packages/s_rcpp.pdf)

**Intro R packages**
[Download slides (PDF)](./slides/topic_R_packages/s_rcpp.pdf)


***

### Lab: Variational Bayes Mixture Model

This lab gets you started on **Problem 2** of HW4: implementing CAVI for a Bayesian Gaussian mixture model.

#### Model

We fit a $K$-component mixture of unit-variance Gaussians with a Gaussian prior on the means:

$$\mu_k \sim N(0, \sigma^2), \quad c_i \sim \text{Categorical}(1/K,\ldots,1/K), \quad y_i \mid c_i, \mu \sim N(c_i^T\mu,\; 1)$$

#### Exercise 1: Set up the function skeleton

Below is a starting skeleton for your CAVI function. Fill in the update steps for $r_{ik}$, $m_k$, $s_k^2$, and the ELBO.

```{r, echo = TRUE, eval = FALSE}
cavi_mixture <- function(y, K, sigma2 = 1, tol = 1e-6, max_iter = 1000) {
  n <- length(y)

  # Initialize responsibilities randomly
  r <- matrix(runif(n * K), nrow = n, ncol = K)
  r <- r / rowSums(r)          # normalize so rows sum to 1

  # Initialize variational parameters for q(mu_k) = N(m_k, s2_k)
  m  <- rnorm(K, mean = mean(y), sd = sd(y))
  s2 <- rep(1, K)

  elbo_vals <- numeric(max_iter)

  for (iter in seq_len(max_iter)) {

    # --- Update q(c_i) ---
    # log_r[i, k] = y[i] * m[k] - 0.5 * (m[k]^2 + s2[k])
    log_r <- outer(y, m) - 0.5 * outer(rep(1, n), m^2 + s2)
    # log-sum-exp normalization
    log_r <- log_r - apply(log_r, 1, function(x) log(sum(exp(x - max(x)))) + max(x))
    r <- exp(log_r)

    # --- Update q(mu_k) ---
    # s2[k]  = 1 / (sum_i r[i,k] + 1/sigma2)
    # m[k]   = s2[k] * sum_i r[i,k] * y[i]
    nk  <- colSums(r)
    s2  <- 1 / (nk + 1 / sigma2)
    m   <- s2 * colSums(r * y)

    # --- Compute ELBO ---
    # expected log-likelihood
    ell <- sum(r * (outer(y, m) - 0.5 * outer(rep(1, n), m^2 + s2)))
    # entropy of q(c)
    ent_c <- -sum(r * log(r + 1e-300))
    # KL[ N(m_k, s2_k) || N(0, sigma2) ]
    kl_mu <- sum(0.5 * ((m^2 + s2) / sigma2 - 1 + log(sigma2) - log(s2)))
    elbo_vals[iter] <- ell + ent_c - kl_mu

    # --- Check convergence ---
    if (iter > 1 && abs(elbo_vals[iter] - elbo_vals[iter - 1]) < tol) {
      elbo_vals <- elbo_vals[seq_len(iter)]
      break
    }
  }

  list(
    m        = m,
    s2       = s2,
    r        = r,
    clusters = apply(r, 1, which.max),
    elbo     = elbo_vals
  )
}
```

#### Exercise 2: Test on simulated data

Simulate data from a 2-component mixture and check that CAVI recovers the true means. Plot the ELBO over iterations to verify it increases monotonically.

```{r, echo = TRUE, eval = FALSE}
set.seed(123)
y_sim <- c(rnorm(50, mean = -3), rnorm(50, mean = 3))

fit <- cavi_mixture(y_sim, K = 2, sigma2 = 10)

# check recovered means
fit$m

# ELBO should increase monotonically
plot(fit$elbo, type = "l", xlab = "Iteration", ylab = "ELBO")
```

#### Exercise 3: Apply to Old Faithful

Apply your function to the Old Faithful waiting times (used in the EM lecture) with $K = 2$. Compare the cluster means to what you obtained from the Gibbs sampler in Problem 1.

```{r, echo = TRUE, eval = FALSE}
data(faithful)
fit_faithful <- cavi_mixture(faithful$waiting, K = 2, sigma2 = 100)
fit_faithful$m
table(fit_faithful$clusters)
```

***

:::
